ddls:
CREATE TABLE "companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each company */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the company */,
  "status_label" TEXT /* Status of the company (e.g., alive, bankrupt) */,
  "industry_id" INTEGER /* ID of the industry the company belongs to */,
  "sector" TEXT /* Sector the company operates in */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the company is based */,
  "date_of_incorporation" TEXT /* Date when the company was incorporated */,
  "market_capitalization" REAL /* Market capitalization of the company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "financial_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each financial data entry */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* ID of the company the financial data belongs to */,
  "year_id" INTEGER /* ID of the year the financial data is for */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Currency of the financial data */,
  "fiscal_year_end_date" TEXT /* Fiscal year-end date for the financial data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_financial_data_company_id FOREIGN KEY ("company_id") REFERENCES companies ("company_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_financial_data_year_id FOREIGN KEY ("year_id") REFERENCES years ("year_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "financial_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each financial metric */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the financial metric */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the financial metric */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of financial metric (e.g., revenue, expense) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "financial_data_metrics" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the financial data */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the financial metric */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the financial metric for the financial data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id", "metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "financial_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each financial report */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* ID of the company the financial report belongs to */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of financial report (e.g., balance sheet, income statement) */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the financial report was generated */,
  "report_file" TEXT /* File path to the financial report */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the financial report (e.g., PDF, Excel) */,
  "report_status" TEXT /* Status of the financial report (e.g., draft, final) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_financial_reports_company_id FOREIGN KEY ("company_id") REFERENCES companies ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, consultant, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "job_function" TEXT /* Job function of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the financial data */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the financial data being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the financial data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_method" TEXT /* Method of access (e.g., web, mobile app) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES financial_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "industries" (
  "industry_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each industry */,
  "industry_name" TEXT /* Name of the industry */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the industry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("industry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "years" (
  "year_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each year */,
  "year_name" TEXT /* Name of the year */,
  "year_type" TEXT /* Type of year (e.g., fiscal year, calendar year) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("year_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "company_industries" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* ID of the company */,
  "industry_id" INTEGER /* ID of the industry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id", "industry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "company_years" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* ID of the company */,
  "year_id" INTEGER /* ID of the year */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id", "year_id")
)
